name: Paul Davis
subject: Remembering my dad
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paul Davis for Kansas
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/x-qWNLhM4dtS9Oc79veJN8xYV-h80w_erayieUdFpZA=/SM611khEK6BGYgbQ7xTxgkQrDQgR_fiU86mOPKEPjAo=)
Dear Peter,

Many of you may know that I lost my dad earlier this year to complications from Parkinson's disease. This is our first Father's Day without him, which makes it a little harder than usual. However, I am still grateful to spend the day with my daughter, Caroline, and I take comfort in the opportunity to reflect on what an incredible man my father was.

Dad spent his career teaching people to be city managers, hospital administrators and just plain good citizens. He had a strong belief in public service and the conviction that government could be a tool to help improve people's lives. He instilled that belief into me.

I often was asked if I ever took a class from my Dad and I would reply "yes, it's called life."

---

One of the most difficult things I ever had to do was to tell him that I had lost on election night in 2014. It was gut wrenching because I knew that my Dad believed in me more than anyone else possibly could.

While I won't be able to call him this November, I'm still determined to redeem that moment with him. That's why, every day, I will continue working toward a goal that I hope will honor his legacy, that will give my daughter a brighter future as well.

I hope you are enjoying this special day with the dads in your life,

Paul

name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: Happy Father's Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Klobuchar for Minnesota
body:
Hi,

Happy Father's Day to all our favorite fathers, especially my two personal favorites -- my husband John and my dad Jim.

My dad taught me that service matters and that the journey up the mountain is so much lighter if your backpack is filled with goodwill and good humor. He knew it was his job to shine a light on the truth. As a journalist, he didn't just tolerate the First Amendment, he embraced it and lived it throughout his life. Here's one of my favorite photos of the two of us on one of our bike rides around Minnesota.

---

And my wonderful husband John is a great dad to our daughter Abigail. Here's a picture of them many years ago on our front steps!

---

To all the fathers out there, thank you. I hope you have a great day celebrating with family!

\-- Amy

I couldn't have done this without you and the rest of my team. But the truth is, the real work starts today.
**Nevada's Senate race is the most important in the country - _The Washington Post_ says what happens here is critical to deciding Senate control. The outcome of this race could decide the future of everything from Obamacare and Medicare to the Supreme Court.**
**[If you care about having a senator who will fight for YOU and won't just rubber-stamp Donald Trump's destructive agenda, then I need you to pitch in $5 immediately>>](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd7bf/4fbb2af3/7f8eb827/5e8babf0/3296146100/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoianJfZnItZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azc3My1mci1uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jr_fr-email?refcode=jtk773-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**

subject: 500 days. Can you believe it?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican National Committee
body:
I want to hear what you think. |
---
| | | | | | | **[Authorized By Trump Headquarters](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=af9242e4f394fcdce81a0d70b6d4b7471b094915620943191dc03a0eb96999afb05cede6c0747242295111c91588445e6c1207ce1cd31a5c)**

Friend,

It's been over 500 days since I took the Oath of Office and promised you we would Make America Great Again!

It's been my great honor to fight for you. But because our mission has been about you, I want to hear your thoughts and opinions.

**[Please take the First 500 Days Approval Survey.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=7801669f57ccd25c11a62d2c64add4423165a3864ab66bce58931d7f03ca9c69115fcfbf6d05193677bd5df22ffc30a41c5d4731786b425a)**

Because of your support, Friend, we have....
* Put ISIS on the run, and sent a message to our adversaries that terrorism and threats against our national security will no longer be tolerated

* Initiated historic talks with North Korea to denuclearize the Korean peninsula and achieve peace and global stability

* Enacted sweeping tax reform, putting hard earned money back in the pockets of Americans

* Brought the unemployment numbers to a record low, tying the lowest rate since 1969 Reduced harmful regulations on American industry that had been crippling the U.S. Economy since the Obama administration

* Strengthened our borders like never before: criminals are now being stopped at the border, harmful drugs are being seized, and America is getting safer

....and so much more.

No longer is the government working against the American People. Now, with your help, I am putting its power back into your hands.

But what matters most is that YOU approve of the work we've done to Make America Great Again!

**[So please, make it your priority to take my First 500 Days Approval Survey today to let me know what you think of the job we've done so far.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=7801669f57ccd25cd2f6c591e9fcc94474b0b429a5636cd0dbacd2155d6b0d96fdd22b0c0fbd078126418852a80c5b752f2a27ea5594ba4b)**

subject: Thanks, Dad
body:
|
---
[ ](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff3a/767134fb/5f5761f6/433831c6/3246748930/VEsH/ "http://www.donnaforprincegeorges.org/")

| | Hi Friend, On Father's Day, I think about my Dad, who served in the Air Force for nearly 30 years while raising a family. He taught me the importance of service to nation and service to community. He reminded me to always stand up for what I believe in and fight for what is right, even when it isn't popular. The election is approaching, and it is your opportunity to use your vote to elect leaders who will stand up for our most vulnerable communities. Prince George's County voters have the opportunity to vote early until June 21st from 10 am to 8 pm. And the June 26th primary is just 10 days away. Make sure your fathers, uncles, brothers, and father figures join you at the polls. name: Tina Smith
subject: My Dad Harlan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
I hope you'll take a couple minutes to read. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4b39b/c1f7015/6821963d/1142d610/2530275350/VEsH/ "https://tinaforminnesota.com/") Friend, My Dad Harlan turned 88 last week, and right before his birthday he traveled to Washington to hear my first speech on the Senate Floor. If you had been there, you would have seen him there in the Senate gallery, leaning forward to hear the proceedings, tall and dignified, paying close attention. Here's what I see when I look at Dad. I see the guy who took me into the mountains with our sleeping bags and fishing rods when I was about 8. It was a thrilling adventure. I thought we had hiked for miles, though it was probably only a fraction the distance. I see the Dad who told me when I was growing up that I could do anything, but I better be sure I did what I wanted to do, not what someone else told me. Imagine if that was true for every little girl. I see the man who whistles Dave Brubeck while he shaves. Who wrote two books and, with my mother, built a straw bale house (after he turned sixty). I see the guy who cries when he listens to music and laughs at my jokes every time. And I laugh right back. Happy Father's Day Dad, every day. I can't wait to see what you do next! Tina | **Proud progressive. A fierce advocate for Minnesota. U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for a seat that CNN's calling "ground zero in the 2018 midterms." name: Andrew Gillum
subject: A better future
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3054/9e00690/4619bd0a/24022d6d/1200123163/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180615_FathDy_fnd_gq_act__AG&source=em18_180615_FathDy_fnd_gq_act__AG")
Friends,

As a dad, my life's goal is to ensure that my three beautiful children grow up knowing that their world is one of endless opportunity. I want them to know that with hard work and a good education, they can do anything.
---
What I want for my children is not fundamentally different than what I wish for all of Florida's children. That's why I'm fighting for public schools, affordable healthcare, and common sense gun reform. So this Father's Day, I ask that we reflect on the role each of us has in society to create a better world for our children and what we can do together to create a better future.

To all my fellow fathers, and all who are celebrating -- Happy Father's Day!

Thank you,

Andrew

name: Martin Heinrich
subject: the bedrock of our families
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE
body:
|
---

| | | Friend,

To all the fathers and father figures out there, I hope you are enjoying a special day with your loved ones.

On a day when we celebrate fathers and enjoy time with our families, I cannot help thinking about ways we must do more to support families. **[One of these ways is to make sure all of our families have access to quality, affordable health care.](http://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/m/4b75b9e8/43d8135f/60a7d639/5100ad10/1681321408/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg3MTAzIn0=/ "https://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/s/stand-up-for-health-care-coverage?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=1+-+One+of+these+ways+is+to+make+sure+all+of&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1&source=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1")**

As Republicans have worked to make health care less accessible, more of us have had to worry not only about our health itself, but also our ability to get the care and coverage we need for ourselves and our families. **[With renewed attacks on basic protections like coverage for pre-existing conditions, it is more important than ever that we stand together to demand quality, affordable health care.](http://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/m/4b75b9e8/43d8135f/60a7d639/5100ad13/1681321408/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg3MTAzIn0=/ "https://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/s/stand-up-for-health-care-coverage?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=2+-+With+renewed+attacks+on+basic+protection&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1&source=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1")**

Accessible, affordable health care is the bedrock of what keeps our families healthy and secure. Parents shouldn't have to worry about if they will be able to afford their child's next doctor's appointment.

[**I promise I will continue to fight for health care for all so that we can all spend more time with our loved ones and less time worrying. I hope you can take a moment today to join me in saying you believe health care is vital for our families, and you support making health care more -- not less -- accessible.**](http://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/m/4b75b9e8/43d8135f/60a7d639/5100ad12/1681321408/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg3MTAzIn0=/ "https://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/s/stand-up-for-health-care-coverage?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=3+-+I+promise+I+will+continue+to+fight+for+h&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1&source=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_60DD_180617_1_1")

Happy Father's Day,

Martin

"" As Republicans have worked to make health care less accessible, more of us have had to worry not only about our health itself, but also our ability to get the care and coverage we need for ourselves and our families. "" Accessible, affordable health care is the bedrock of what keeps our families healthy and secure. Parents shouldn't have to worry about if they will be able to afford their child's next doctor's appointment. "" Happy Father's Day, Martin | PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE Help us spread the word by forwarding this email to 3 friends! Receive this email from a friend? name: Zephyr Teachout
subject: If we are silent on this issue, we are complicit.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Zephyr for NY
body:
We must confront the horror happening right now.
#
Peter -
Today should be a day full of love and celebration of dads across our country. But this year, Father's Day is just not as joyous, because in America, in our name, children are being torn from their families and forced to sit, without their parents, in a cage.
We must confront the horror happening right now -- children being ripped away from parents by border guards. The crying, the fear, the complete sense of powerlessness from parents and children alike.
The moral fury of our country, grounded in humanity, can not let these outrageous family separations continue. We must overcome their politics of fear and scarcity and calculation with a politics of love and righteous anger and justice.
But that means we must make our voices heard on this issue. If we are silent on this issue, we are complicit.
**[My Congressmember, John Faso, is a Republican in a swing district and has not publicly condemned the President for his policy. Faso needs to hear from us now. Will you join me in calling him and other Republicans in Congress to demand they clearly condemn Trump for his policy of family separation? Republicans breaking with the President mean a lot right now.](tel:202-224-3121)**
As you may have heard, my husband Nick and I are expecting this year, and with every kick, I'm reminded of the unbreakable bond between a parent and their child. But while celebrating the love and joy this child will bring to our lives, today I keep thinking about the inhumane, heartless policy separating families.
This administration is using children as a gross, political tactic. We must draw on our love for ALL children as our inspiration to fight back.
They use money to elect people who will be silent in the face of immorality; we come together as a grassroots force.
We the people have been and always will be more powerful than any one person or party if we are united.
This policy is not who we are, and we cannot be silent during this crisis. Please join us and speak out against this evil.
\- Zephyr

name: Marena Casey
subject: Happy Father's Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate Inc
body:
[](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6610/51dbcc2a/50bf9ea/3a5f109/890095710/VEsH/ "https://bobcasey.com/") Hello!

I wanted to take a minute today to tell you about my dad, Bob Casey. I'm the youngest of four daughters, which means my dad is really outnumbered in our house, but he's always taken it in stride. Growing up, the CDs in his car were a mix of Bruce Springsteen and Michelle Branch, and he really didn't care which one we listened to.

I want every voter to know that my dad is a good listener, and not just to me and my sisters. When we run into people on the street who tell him about their problems and their fears, I know that the concern he shows them is real. Those conversations stick with him, and when we talk about why he's working so hard on different pieces of legislation, it often comes back to those conversations with Pennsylvanians who need a helping hand.

This November will be my first time voting for my dad, and I'm really excited. Not because he's my dad, but because I know just how hard he works for the people of Pennsylvania. I've watched as he's fought to protect affordable health care, Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security. He protects every child's right to a good public education. He passed landmark legislation to fight campus sexual assault, and I've seen the effects of that at my own school. The bottom line is that he gets things done, and we need his voice in the Senate.

I'm proud to share my dad with the rest of Pennsylvania. On behalf of my sisters and my family, Happy Father's Day to everyone celebrating, and thanks for your support.

\-- Marena [DONATE](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180617_fr1_s2&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

subject: Father’s Day

body:
| Hi Friend, Today is Father’s Day, so I asked Austin’s campaign manager if I could send an email to you this weekend. I am the proud father of my son, Austin. Growing up, Austin was a joy to raise. Always happy and full of life. He loved playing baseball and was a great fishing buddy. He always caught the most fish. When he got into high school he was in all the school plays and starred in most of them. The teachers would tell me and his Mom, “we wish you could bottle up how you raised your boys”. When Austin told me that he was running for Senate, I told him I had his back. I couldn’t be more proud of the man my son has become. [**This Father’s Day, it would make my day if you would chip into my son’s campaign for U.S. Senate! Maybe you can donate $20 or $50? Please click here or on the image below to donate.**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.anedot.com%2Faustinpetersen%2Ffathers_day%3Futm_campaign%3D061718_fr%26source_code%3Demail%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26first_name%3D%26last_name%3D%26street%3D%26zip%3D%26city%3D%26state%3D%26employer%3D%26occupation%3D%26phone%3D&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=061718_fr&n=1)****[****](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.anedot.com%2Faustinpetersen%2Ffathers_day%3Futm_campaign%3D061718_fr%26source_code%3Demail%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26first_name%3D%26last_name%3D%26street%3D%26zip%3D%26city%3D%26state%3D%26employer%3D%26occupation%3D%26phone%3D&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=061718_fr&n=2)**** I urge you all to do what you can financially to get Austin across the finish line. I am counting on your help to elect Austin to the Senate. [**Seriously, even $3 or $5 makes a difference. Please donate $5, $10, $20, $50, $75, $100, $150, $250 or whatever! Join the movement.**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.anedot.com%2Faustinpetersen%2Ffathers_day%3Futm_campaign%3D061718_fr%26source_code%3Demail%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26first_name%3D%26last_name%3D%26street%3D%26zip%3D%26city%3D%26state%3D%26employer%3D%26occupation%3D%26phone%3D&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=061718_fr&n=3) Here is the link to donate: [**https://secure.anedot.com/austinpetersen/fathers_day**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.anedot.com%2Faustinpetersen%2Ffathers_day%3Futm_campaign%3D061718_fr%26source_code%3Demail%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26first_name%3D%26last_name%3D%26street%3D%26zip%3D%26city%3D%26state%3D%26employer%3D%26occupation%3D%26phone%3D&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=061718_fr&n=4) And happy Father’s Day to all the dads out there :) Thank you, Friend. body:

---

| | Hi Peter,

**This Father 's Day, I want to take a minute to tell you about my husband, Joe Donnelly.** You might not know that Joe served on the Mishawaka Marian High School Board. We wanted to make sure our children, along with every child, received the best possible education -- so Joe decided to serve. | []()
---
**Joe got into public service for the right reasons, and he 's still in it for the right reasons.** Joe ran for the Senate to be a voice for everyone in Indiana, and these days he works on everything from helping families who have been touched by the opioid epidemic to protecting health insurance for people with pre-existing conditions. He's there for veterans and people with terminal illnesses who want a shot at experimental treatments. He supports the right of LGBTQ families to live free from discrimination.

I couldn't be prouder of him -- and I know our two kids, Molly and Joe, Jr., feel the same way. It's been a blessing to watch Joe dedicate himself to serving others in our community, and I'm so grateful for the example he's set for our children. We need more public servants like Joe in Washington.

**Joe is running for re-election this year, and on this Father 's Day, he wants to hear directly from you. Will you leave Joe a note telling him what you think is the most important thing he can do to help Hoosier families right now?**

Thank you,
Jill Donnelly

name: Mel Hall
subject: My dad
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mel Hall for Congress
body:
Friend,

My father was a farmer and a union carpenter before he started his own general contracting business.

He and my mother raised us on the family farm, where they taught my three sisters and me early on the importance of a hard day's work. Growing up, I fed the calves and chickens, hung drywall, and worked as a hod carrier for bricklayers.

My parents taught us the old expression, "What you do speaks so loud I can't hear what you say." I try to live that creed in everything I do.

It's what inspired me to spend seven years serving as a Methodist minister in Detroit's poorest and most densely-populated neighborhood.

And it's what led me as a business leader to focus on improving patient care and taking care of our employees, raising wages and making sure everyone received equal benefits.

**This Father 's Day, I'm running to serve Indiana's Second congressional district because my dad taught me to lead through my actions. Will you take action too and share this photo of my family, along with a message about why I'm running, to help spread the word?**
